explanation:
The natural language describes that in PSV mode, the inspiration phase should end when either the inspiration pressure peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or when a maximum inspiratory time (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) elapses, after which the expiration phase begins. The appropriate encoding is that whenever it is PSV mode AND currently in the inspiration phase, the phase should end (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) as soon as either the peak is reached OR the time limit has passed. This can be formulated as: 'Globally, whenever PSVMode and inspiratoryPhase are true, if either P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp or inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime is true, then inspiratoryPhaseEnd must be true in the next step.'

This matches the LTL template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where:
bool_exp1 = PSVMode
bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhase
bool_exp3 = (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) -> inspiratoryPhaseEnd

But to encode the logic precisely, since the 'end' happens if either peak or time occurs, we set (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) as the trigger, and guarantee inspiratoryPhaseEnd in the next step.

N_DURATION is not required here, so set to null.

The natural language requirement states that, in PSV mode, the inspiration phase lasts until either the inspiration peak is reached or the maximum inspiratory time (max_insp_time_psv) elapses, and after that, the expiration phase starts. This can be formalized such that whenever the system is in the inspiratory phase, eventually (within a bounded duration) either the inspiration peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the inspiratory time limit is reached (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime). Immediately afterwards, the expiration phase should begin (expiratoryPhaseStart). This behavior describes the outcome trigger for ending the inspiratory phase. Therefore, the property captures that in every inspiratory phase, reaching either of the termination conditions guarantees the next state is the start of expiration.

The natural language requirement specifies that in PSV mode, inspiration should end if either the pressure peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or if the maximum inspiratory time is reached (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime); after that, expiration phase should begin (expiratoryPhaseStart). LTL specification must formalize the following: while in inspiration (inspiratoryPhase), it continues until either of the above conditions triggers the end of inspiration, then expiration phase starts. The most direct way is to express: whenever it becomes the case that either P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp or inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime becomes true, in the next step, expiration phase is started. This is a standard 'rising-edge' property and matches template 20 (index 20):

(G (((! (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime)) & (X (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime))) -> (X expiratoryPhaseStart))) & ((P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) -> expiratoryPhaseStart)

Here, bool_exp2 is '(P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime)', and bool_exp3 is 'expiratoryPhaseStart', meaning every time one of the possible inspiration-ending events is newly satisfied, expiration phase starts on the next transition. This captures the requirement that inspiration ends when either the peak is reached or the max time expires, after which expiration starts.

The property expresses the following: During the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhase), the phase continues until either the pressure peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the max inspiration time for PSV is reached (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime); that is, the inspiratory phase ends and the expiratory phase starts immediately after either of these events. This is captured by specifying that, whenever the system is in the inspiration phase, it is always the case that when either peak pressure or max time is reached, the inspiration phase will end in the next step (X inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and the expiration will start. This is implemented by the implication: whenever inspiratoryPhase & (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) holds, then in the next step, inspiratoryPhaseEnd & expiratoryPhaseStart must hold. This captures the behavior described in PSV mode, in particular the transition from inspiration to expiration based on the peak or time timeout.

The input natural language specifies the logic for Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode: the inspiratory phase should continue until either the inspiration pressure peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the maximum inspiratory time is exceeded (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime), whichever happens first. After that, the inspiratory phase must end (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and the expiration phase must begin (expiratoryPhaseStart). In LTL, this can best be described by stating that whenever the inspiratory phase is active, if either the inspiration peak is reached OR the maximum inspiratory time is up, then in the next moment, the inspiratory phase ends AND the expiration phase begins. This is captured as a response property on the inspiratoryPhase being active: when (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp OR inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) is true, then (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & expiratoryPhaseStart) must follow in the next step.

The input describes that in PSV mode, the inspiration phase should end when the inspiration peak (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) is reached or if the inspiration time exceeds the max allowed (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime). Immediately after this, the expiration phase begins (expiratoryPhaseStart). This is best captured by stating: whenever in the inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhase), if either the pressure peak is reached or the maximum inspiratory time is exceeded, then at the next time step the expiration phase begins. This temporal dependency is a classic 'if condition now, then next...' form. Therefore, we use template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhase & (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) and bool_exp3 = expiratoryPhaseStart. N_DURATION is not needed here.

This property formalizes that in PSV Mode, the inspiration phase continues until either the peak inspiratory pressure is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or the maximum inspiratory time (max_insp_time_psv, here represented by inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) is reached. As soon as either condition becomes true, the inspiratory phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and the expiratory phase begins (expiratoryPhaseStart). The template expresses that whenever PSVMode is active and the inspiratory phase is running, reaching either the pressure peak or time-out causes the inspiratory phase to end on the next step, and the expiration phase to begin.

To reflect the requirement that, in PSV Mode, the inspiratory phase ends either when the inspiration peak is reached or when the max inspiration time is reached, we encode that whenever the system is in PSVMode and the inspiratory phase is active, then eventually either the peak pressure is reached or the max inspiratory time is reached. Separately, we globally insist that whenever either condition for ending inspiration is met, the expiratory phase must begin in the next step. We split this into two formulas using available LTL templates: (1) any time PSVMode and inspiratoryPhase holds, one of the two conditions for inspiration ending will happen within N_DURATION; (2) globally, when either pressure peak or time limit is reached, next state is expiratoryPhaseStart. This approach respects the restriction that each LTL template only takes a valid boolean formula without temporal operators for its atomic propositions and matches the requirements specified in the natural language.

The property states that whenever Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSV) is active (PSVMode), then globally, as long as we are in the inspiratory phase, the phase will end as soon as the inspiratory peak is reached (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp) or if the duration of inspiration exceeds the maximum allowed inspiration time in PSV mode (inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime). Only then does the expiration phase begin, which is indicated by expiratoryPhaseStart. We encode the end condition with a next-step requirement (since phase may end on the next tick after logic is triggered): globally, if PSVMode and inspiratoryPhase is active, then as soon as (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp | inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) holds, the next state must satisfy (expiratoryPhaseStart). This captures the natural language: inspiration lasts until peak is reached but no later than max_insp_time_psv, after which expiration begins. N_DURATION is not strictly needed for this liveness/next-step logic, so we set it to 2 as a dummy value.

The natural language states: In PSV mode, the inspiratory phase lasts until the inspiration peak is reached ('inspiration peak is reached' = 'P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp'), but the inspiration cannot last longer than a maximum time limit ('max_insp_time_psv is over' = 'inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime'). After inspiration ends, expiration starts ('expiration phase begins' = 'expiratoryPhaseStart'). So, the end condition for inspiratory phase is either the pressure peak is reached or max_insp_time_psv expires. The start of expiration is then tightly connected to this end condition. In LTL, we express: whenever the system is in inspiratory phase in PSV mode, if (P_insp_greaterThanOrEqual_MaxP_insp OR inspClock_greaterThanOrEqual_inspiratoryTime) happens, then at the next moment, the expiratory phase begins. This is a classic 'response in next step after a disjunct condition in a mode' property. Thus, use template 11.
